methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pasqal is a pre-IPO quantum computing company founded by Alain Aspect and Georges-Olivier Reymond, having raised €340M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises a modest peer universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including Quantinuum and Infleqtion.

Traditional peer-median valuation is not applicable here. The narrow quantum computing peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables—zero datapoints meet the minimum threshold of three companies with aligned stage and valuation tier. A broader sector median would conflate early-stage venture-backed firms with pre-IPO or public entities, introducing material distortion. The heterogeneity of quantum modalities, commercialization timelines, and burn profiles across the peer set further limits comparable company utility.

Given these constraints, a defensible valuation range cannot be derived from the data provided. Triangulation would require either (1) revenue or EBITDA multiples from the public subset, (2) precedent transaction data for acquired quantum firms, or (3) late-stage funding round pricing. Without these inputs, the analysis is incomplete. A full valuation would depend on Pasqal's specific financial metrics, technology differentiation within the quantum landscape, and customer traction relative to the named peers—none of which are supplied here.
